summary: Revenue-team AI agents for customer understanding, prep, follow-up, and CRM work.
editor_note: Revenue teams already have call recorders, CRM fields, and dashboards; the missing layer is action. Airspeed uses multi-model agents, customer memory, and integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, Gmail, Outlook, Zoom, and Teams to prep calls, draft follow-ups, and keep CRM clean. Unlike alerts, it tries to close the workflow loop. The risk is proving buyers trust agents inside 200-customer revenue operations.

product_evidence.claim: The official site describes agents for call prep, follow-ups, CRM hygiene, coaching plans, and integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Gmail, Outlook, Zoom, and Teams.
The Series A story says Airspeed is approaching 200 customers and frames the product around context layers, sub-agent orchestration, and Customer 360.

market_context.primary_user: Revenue, sales, customer-success, and go-to-market teams with scattered customer context.
market_context.differentiation: Airspeed sells an action layer that uses commercial memory and connected GTM tools rather than another sales-intelligence dashboard.
market_context.why_now: The June 2026 Series A follows fast customer growth as revenue teams test agentic workflows against CRM and meeting data.

risks.claim: The open question is whether enterprise buyers will allow autonomous agents to act inside revenue operations instead of keeping them in draft-only mode.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
